title: rostellularia heterocarpoides
intro: Rostellularia heterocarpoides, also known as the 'native lavender', is a flowering plant native to Australia, commonly found in the eastern regions. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory problems due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Rostellularia heterocarpoides include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to relieve congestion and promote relaxation.
